Package com.ducksabervn.projects.ketchup.main

Class KetchupMain

java.lang.Object
javafx.application.Application
com.ducksabervn.projects.ketchup.main.KetchupMain
public class KetchupMain extends javafx.application.Application
The main entry point of the Ketchup application. Responsible for three sequential bootstrap tasks before handing control to the UI layer: 1. Initializing the file system via FileSystemInitializer, ensuring the application directory and all required CSV files exist. 2. Loading all movie records from MOVIES.csv into MovieRepository. 3. Loading all credential records from USER_CREDENTIALS.csv into CredentialRepository. Once bootstrapping is complete, LoginUIController.initialize() is called on the JavaFX Application Thread (already the case inside start()). The Nimbus look-and-feel setup from the original Swing version is removed as it is Swing-specific and has no equivalent in JavaFX.

  • Method Details

    • init

    • start

      public void start(javafx.stage.Stage primaryStage)
      JavaFX application entry point. Called on the JavaFX Application Thread after the toolkit is initialized. The primaryStage provided by the framework is unused here because each screen manages its own Stage. This keeps the controller pattern consistent with the rest of the migrated UI layer. Any IOException thrown during file system initialization or CSV reading is caught and shown via a blocking error Alert before the application exits.
      Specified by:
      start in class javafx.application.Application
      Parameters:
      primaryStage - the initial Stage provided by the JavaFX runtime; not used by this application

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Standard Java main method. Delegates to Application.launch() which initializes the JavaFX toolkit and calls start() on the JavaFX Application Thread. Replaces the original Swing bootstrap sequence: UIManager.setLookAndFeel(...) and SwingUtilities.invokeLater(...).
      Parameters:
      args - command-line arguments; not used by this application
